headline: Washington's export-control order forced Anthropic to pull Fable 5 and Mythos 5 offline worldwide, the day's signal that governments now treat frontier AI as a national-security asset.

emerging_trends.subject: AI Governance
emerging_trends.text: Governments now wield export-control and national-security authority directly over frontier AI, as Washington's order forcing Anthropic to suspend two models shows.

emerging_trends.subject: AI as Auditor
emerging_trends.text: Security researchers increasingly turn large language models on hard targets, and Claude Opus 4.8 surfaced a critical Zcash cryptographic flaw fast.

emerging_trends.subject: Supply Chain
emerging_trends.text: Ecosystem maintainers are hardening defaults against poisoned packages, and npm 12's move to disable install scripts marks the latest structural fix.

emerging_trends.subject: Insider Risk
emerging_trends.text: Trusted access keeps turning into a weapon, from a jailed ex-school-district technician to a decade-long hijack of an organization's authentication stack.

topics.title: Anthropic Pulls Fable 5 and Mythos 5 Under U.S. Order
topics.latest_developments: Anthropic now disputes the order's basis—calling the cited jailbreak narrow and the capability widely available elsewhere—even as it complies, and officials frame the worldwide suspension as an export-control measure.
topics.summary: The Trump administration ordered Anthropic at 5:21 p.m. ET on June 13 to block all foreign nationals, inside or outside the United States, from using Claude Fable 5 and Mythos 5, citing national security. Anthropic disabled both models for every user worldwide to comply. The company contests the rationale and points to similar capabilities in rival models. Enterprises that lean on these models for coding or analysis must fall back to earlier Claude versions or alternatives.

sources.title: US Gov asks Anthropic to ban 'foreign national' access to Fable, Mythos
Anthropic Says It Has Taken Its Latest AI Models Offline to Comply With New Export Controls
U.S. Orders Anthropic to Suspend Fable 5 and Mythos 5 Access for Foreign Nationals

topics.title: NPM 12 Disables Dependency Scripts by Default
topics.latest_developments: The coming npm 12 release will stop running install scripts from dependencies unless a developer explicitly allows them, closing a path supply-chain attackers have long abused.
topics.summary: Lifecycle scripts that fire automatically during npm install hand attackers a foothold to run code the moment a poisoned package lands. The npm 12 release flips the default so these scripts stay dormant unless a developer permits them. JavaScript teams and CI pipelines should test builds against the new behavior and add allowlists for the few packages that genuinely need install-time scripts.

sources.title: NPM 12 Will Change Script Execution Behavior to Prevent Supply Chain Attacks

topics.title: Claude Opus Finds Critical Zcash Orchard Flaw
topics.latest_developments: Researcher Taylor Hornby, hired by the Zcash team and working with Claude Opus 4.8, found a critical vulnerability in the Orchard shielded-transaction pool on May 29, and the team has now fixed it.
topics.summary: The Orchard pool, introduced in 2022, is Zcash's newest privacy system for shielded transactions. Hornby, whom the Zcash team paid to hunt exactly this class of bug, surfaced the critical flaw quickly using Claude Opus 4.8, and the team patched it. Zcash holders should update to the fixed software. The case shows large language models accelerating serious cryptographic auditing.

sources.title: Critical Zcash Vulnerability Found and Fixed

topics.title: Ex-IT Worker Jailed for Year-Long School District Attack
topics.latest_developments: A federal court sentenced a former Iowa school district IT employee to 21 months in prison for a prolonged attack that deleted accounts and disrupted classrooms.
topics.summary: After leaving the district, the former IT worker kept access and ran a sustained campaign that wiped user accounts, interrupted classroom operations, and caused tens of thousands of dollars in damage. The 21-month sentence underscores the danger of lingering credentials after staff depart. Organizations should revoke every account and key the day an employee leaves.

sources.title: Ex-school district employee jailed for hacks on former employer

games.recap: Spencer Horwitz hit by pitch with the bases loaded to lift the Pirates past the Marlins, 3-2
Meyer outduels Skenes, allows one run in six innings as Marlins top Pirates 4-2

business_politics.title: Iran Deal Complete, Hormuz Reopens
business_politics.latest_developments: Trump declared the deal with Iran "now complete" Sunday and ordered the U.S. naval blockade lifted, with a signing ceremony set for June 19 in Switzerland, an advance from Saturday, when Tehran still threatened to walk over an Israeli strike on Beirut.
business_politics.summary: The United States and Iran agreed to end three months of war, extend the ceasefire 60 days, reopen the Strait of Hormuz, and begin nuclear negotiations. Oil flows resume after a shock that had drained global commercial and strategic inventories toward minimum operating levels; analysts at CBA see Brent falling toward $80 a barrel by year-end if the strait stays open. Asian currencies and Japanese government bond futures firmed on the news.

sources.title: U.S. and Iran Say They Have Reached a Deal to Stop Fighting
Iran and US agree deal to open Strait of Hormuz and extend ceasefire
What is in the US-Iran deal?

business.title: Skill Games Defended in Lottery Debate
business.latest_developments: In a TribLive opinion piece, Ryan Sprankle argued Pennsylvania's skill games and the state lottery succeed together, contesting the claim that every dollar played on a skill game costs the lottery one.
business.summary: Pennsylvania lawmakers continue to weigh how to regulate and tax skill games, the slot-like terminals that have spread through bars and convenience stores. Sprankle, writing for retailers, contends revenue figures show the two products complement each other and help local businesses.

sources.title: Ryan Sprankle: Skill games and lottery succeed together

business.title: TribLive Spotlights Table Magazine
business.latest_developments: TribLive opened a weekly Pittsburgh Media Partnership series with a profile of Table Magazine, a regional food and culture publication.
business.summary: The recurring feature aims to highlight members of the Pittsburgh Media Partnership and the broader Western Pennsylvania media landscape.

sources.title: Pittsburgh Media Partnership series: Table Magazine

around_town.title: Storms Topple Trees, Cut Power to 37,000
around_town.latest_developments: Severe thunderstorms carrying multiple tornado warnings swept Western Pennsylvania Sunday evening, downing trees and leaving more than 37,000 customers without power before all warnings expired around 8 p.m.
around_town.summary: The line moved in around 5 p.m., toppling trees onto roads and into homes across Beaver, Washington, and neighboring counties. Crews worked through the night to restore service.

sources.title: More than 37K power outages reported in Pittsburgh region following severe storms
Warnings expire as severe weather moves out of Western Pennsylvania

around_town.title: Cooler, Clearer Week Ahead
around_town.latest_developments: A cold front behind Sunday's storms drops highs into the 70s Monday with lows in the 50s, clouds breaking by Tuesday for full sunshine.
around_town.summary: The cooler, drier stretch follows a stormy weekend across the region.

sources.title: Sunday's cold front brings in cooler temperatures for Monday

around_town.title: Pride Celebrations in Freeport, Vandergrift
around_town.latest_developments: Both Alle-Kiski Valley boroughs held Pride Month gatherings Sunday emphasizing acceptance and support.
around_town.summary: From Vandergrift to Freeport, residents marked the month with community events in the heart of the valley.

sources.title: Pride Month celebrations in Freeport, Vandergrift emphasize acceptance, support of being one's true self

events.title: Pennsylvania Firefly Festival
events.latest_developments: NEXTpittsburgh detailed the 14th annual Pennsylvania Firefly Festival, running June 26 and 27 in Tionesta, with guided night viewings already sold out and free daytime programming still open.
events.summary: The festival takes place about 100 miles north of Pittsburgh in the Allegheny National Forest, home to at least 15 firefly species that light up at this time of year. Guided nocturnal sightings are booked; free daytime nature exhibits, music, and activities remain available.

sources.title: What happens at a firefly festival?

events.title: Arts Festival Closes at New Arts Landing Home
events.latest_developments: The Dollar Bank Three Rivers Arts Festival wrapped its 67th run Sunday, its first at the new Arts Landing site in the Cultural District, which the Post-Gazette reports drew warm reviews from vendors and visitors.
events.summary: The free festival, which ran June 11 through 14 in its new downtown home, brought together hundreds of artists and musicians.

sources.title: Three Rivers Arts Festival's new home at Arts Landing seems to be a hit

around_teams.title: Cruz Out 4-6 Weeks, Valdez Recalled
around_teams.latest_developments: The Post-Gazette reports the Pirates expect Oneil Cruz to miss four to six weeks and have recalled Esmerlyn Valdez to fill the roster spot.
around_teams.summary: Cruz's absence reshuffles the Pirates' lineup, with Endy Rodríguez also figuring into the plan during his recovery.

sources.title: With Oneil Cruz expected to miss 4-6 weeks, the Pirates have recalled Esmerlyn Valdez

around_teams.title: Skenes Speaks on MLB Labor Fight
around_teams.latest_developments: In a Post-Gazette feature, Paul Skenes, now near the center of baseball's labor fight over payroll and a possible salary cap, said the players need to dig in ahead of the next collective bargaining agreement.
around_teams.summary: The piece weighs owner Bob Nutting's spending, commissioner Rob Manfred, and the players' union as the sport heads toward a contentious negotiation.

sources.title: Why Paul Skenes, at the center of MLB's labor battle, believes the players need to dig in

around_teams.title: Ten Takeaways From Steelers' Spring
around_teams.latest_developments: The Post-Gazette listed 10 things it learned about coach Mike McCarthy and the Steelers from spring workouts.
around_teams.summary: The rundown covered quarterback Aaron Rodgers, cornerback Joey Porter Jr., receiver DK Metcalf, and running backs Rico Dowdle and Kenneth Gainwell as the offseason program closed.

sources.title: 10 things we learned about Mike McCarthy and the Steelers during spring workouts

around_teams.title: Podcast Weighs Leaguewide QB Questions
around_teams.latest_developments: On the Steelers' SNR Drive, Matt Williamson and Wes Uhler broke down an ESPN piece laying out the quarterback questions facing all 32 NFL teams.
around_teams.summary: The hosts ranked divisions by quarterback talent and placed the Steelers' situation in that leaguewide context.

sources.title: QB Questions for All 32 Teams | SNR Drive | Pittsburgh Steelers

around_teams.title: Williamson Ranks the Top 10 Steelers
around_teams.latest_developments: On SNR Drive, Matt Williamson presented his list of the 10 greatest Steelers of all time and debated it with Wes Uhler.
around_teams.summary: The segment ran through the franchise's deep history of Hall of Fame talent.

sources.title: Matt Williamson's Top 10 Steelers of All-Time | SNR Drive | Pittsburgh Steelers

reading.author: Ed Zitron
reading.title: Premium: The Silicon Valley Bubble (Part 1)
reading.summary: Zitron argues the AI era is nearing its end as OpenAI and Anthropic both file to go public, racing for exit liquidity despite burning billions a year with no path to profitability.

reading.author: Stratechery
reading.title: Fable 5, Anthropic Alignment, AI Tiers
reading.summary: Ben Thompson examines Fable 5 as the public version of Anthropic's Mythos model, judging it highly capable while warning that its release sets troubling precedents around alignment and tiered access.

reading.author: Cal Newport
reading.title: Why Isn't AI Taking Our Jobs?
reading.summary: Newport questions the AI industry's analogy to industrial automation, probing why the technology has yet to eliminate jobs the way machines displaced manual labor.
